name: Prometheus /metrics endpoint
description: Expose a /metrics endpoint in Prometheus text format. Instrument the things that matter: HTTP request count and latency per route, upload bytes received, transcode durations, WebSocket RPC call latency per method, background-worker run durations, and current connected-device count.
acceptance_criteria: Uses github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus for metrics plus promhttp.Handler for the HTTP endpoint
GET /metrics returns the Prometheus exposition format; endpoint is NOT authed (common convention; if an operator wants auth, they can front it with nginx)

Counters/histograms: http_requests_total{method, path, status}, http_request_duration_seconds{method, path}, upload_bytes_total{dongle_id}, transcode_duration_seconds{camera, result}, rpc_call_duration_seconds{method}, rpc_calls_total{method, status}, ws_connected_devices (gauge), worker_run_duration_seconds{worker}
HTTP middleware hooks the counters/histograms via an Echo middleware registered once in main.go
The transcoder, WebSocket RPC caller, and each background worker accept a *Metrics struct in their constructors so they can call .Observe / .Inc -- tests can pass a dummy or real registry
internal/metrics/metrics_test.go asserts the metric names + labels exist after a simulated call
go vet ./... and go build ./... pass cleanly
